nodes.title: Traditional Retrieval
nodes.text: Retrieval Augmented Generation traditionally uses vector embeddings. Embeddings map documents into dense representations. Similarity search then identifies related information. However, vector systems sometimes fail when semantic relationships are subtle or when explicit entity relationships matter.

nodes.title: Vectorless Graph Retrieval
nodes.text: Vectorless retrieval introduces entity extraction, graph construction, relationship mapping, and symbolic navigation. Documents become nodes and concepts become edges. Examples include relationships between attention mechanisms, tool use, multimodal systems, AI agents, and memory architectures. Systems can generate relationship trees connecting entities across many documents.

nodes.title: Knowledge Graph Patterns
nodes.text: Graph systems frequently support document exploration. Nodes can represent concepts, companies, people, or technical artifacts. Traversal enables explanation pathways.

document_description: This document delves into the advanced topics
